channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet the Mexican soldier trying to revamp a musical genre accused of glorifying cartels
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — At a Mexican military base, Captain Eduardo Barrón picks up not a rifle but a microphone. Swaying boot-to-boot, he belts out a song as the sounds of trumpets and accordions roar from a band of a dozen camouflage-clad soldiers.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Asian markets fall as Trump's tariffs roil global trade
item.description: Asian markets plunged on Monday following last week’s two-day meltdown on Wall Street, and President Donald Trump said he won’t back down on the sweeping new tariffs he announced on April 2 that have roiled global trade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Live updates: Trump defends tariffs; Billionaire Trump endorser calls for trade war time-out
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are on defense amid the fallout from President Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy. Markets took a nosedive Thursday following Trump’s announcement of a general 10 percent tariff on goods imported to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Palestinian teenager who died in Israeli prison showed signs of starvation, medical report says
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Starvation was likely the leading cause of death for a Palestinian teenager who died in an Israeli prison, according to an Israeli doctor who observed the autopsy.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US revokes visas for South Sudanese as civil war looms at home
item.description: Here’s a look at South Sudan, whose people had been granted temporary protected status by the U.S. because of insecurity at home. That status expires on May 3.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel walks back its account of the killing of 15 medics in Gaza after video seems to contradict it
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Israeli military backtracked on its account of the killing of 15 Palestinian medics by its forces last month after phone video appeared to contradict its claims that their vehicles did not have emergency signals on when troops opened fire on them in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, WAFAA SHURAFA and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill 32, mostly women and children
item.description: Palestinian health officials say Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip have killed at least 32 people including over a dozen women and children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump tariffs pose danger for vulnerable Republicans
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are being put on defense amid the fallout from President Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: 1 killed in Russian attack on Kyiv as death toll from earlier missile strike rises to 20
item.description: Kyiv's Mayor Vitali Klitschko says one person was was killed as Russian air strikes hit the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canadian police say arrest made after man barricaded himself inside Parliament's east block
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Police said a man was arrested late Saturday after an hours-long lockdown on Parliament Hill in Canada's capital. Investigators said the man gained unauthorized access to the east block of Canada's parliament on Saturday afternoon and barricaded himself inside the building.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea presidential ouster part of Chinese strategy to 'expand its regional influence,' expert says
item.description: One analyst, as well as supporters of South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, says China backed Yoon's ouster in South Korea to weaken U.S. ties and expand its influence by propping up Beijing-friendly political allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Singer Rara Sekar draws inspiration from nature, encourages people to return to simple living
item.description: OXFORD, England (AP) — Rara Sekar closed her eyes in meditation after performing a song that speaks of rays of light that cut through the fog as one political prisoner faced death more than six decades ago.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and VICKRAM SOMBU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kurdish fighters leave northern city in Syria as part of deal with central government
item.description: ALEPPO, Syria (AP) — Scores of U.S.-backed Kurdish fighters left two neighborhoods in Syria’s northern city of Aleppo Friday as part of a deal with the central government in Damascus, which is expanding its authority in the country.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Experts warn Iran’s nuclear double-talk designed to buy time, undermine US pressure
item.description: President Donald Trump is sending both carrots and sticks towards Iran, stepping up military assets in the Middle East while also signaling talks are still on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houthis shoot down 3rd US reaper drone as Trump administration continues daily airstrikes
item.description: The Houthis shot down another U.S. Reaper drone, the third one that has been destroyed by the terror organization since early March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol had a rapid rise in South Korean politics before an abrupt downfall
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol’s political rise was fast: the former star prosecutor clinched South Korea’s presidency only a year after he entered politics.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president removed from office four months after declaring martial law
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was removed form office Friday, four months after he declared martial law, sending the East Asian country into chaos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World leaders react to Trump’s sweeping tariffs: ‘tumultuous times’ ahead
item.description: World leaders had varied reactions to President Donald Trump's tariff announcements, with many pledging counter measures, while others hope to negotiate a removal of the import taxes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio demands NATO pony up, dismisses 'hysteria' over US role under Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NATO allies they must make hard choices and increase defense spending to 5% to bolster capabilities in face of emboldened adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Burma earthquake death toll rises to 3,085, humanitarian crisis deepens
item.description: More than 3,000 people have died from the 7.7 magnitude earthquake that struck Burma last week, which has sent the country into a deeper humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli defense minister says military is expanding its operation in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday, as airstrikes on the southern city of Khan Younis killed 17 people overnight, according to hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel prepares to move deeper into Gaza and orders Rafah evacuated
item.description: The Israeli military indicated on Monday it could soon launch another major offensive in Rafah and ordered most of the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip evacuated. Israel ended its ceasefire with Hamas and renewed its air and ground war earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French court finds right-wing leader Marine Le Pen guilty of embezzlement
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A French court found Marine Le Pen guilty on Monday in an embezzlement case but didn’t immediately say what her sentence might be and how it might impact the right-wing leader’s political future.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year
item.description: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year, as a deadline for them to voluntarily leave the capital and surrounding areas expired on Monday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, NewsNation
